paper_title: Reconstruction of Relative Sea-Level Changes in Prydz Bay

methods.method_name: Compilation and Re-evaluation of RSL Data
methods.description: This method involves compiling previously published relative sea-level (RSL) data from the Prydz Bay region, re-evaluating them in terms of maximum and minimum sea-level constraints, and recalibrating all radiocarbon dates.

steps.description: Compilation of previously published RSL data from the Larsemann Hills and Vestfold Hills.
steps.searchable_summary: compile RSL data from published sources

steps.description: Re-evaluation of RSL data in terms of maximum and minimum sea-level constraints.
steps.searchable_summary: re-evaluate RSL data for sea-level constraints

methods.method_name: Validation of RSL Constraint in Rauer Islands
methods.description: This method validates a potential submarine RSL constraint in the Rauer Islands using high-resolution diatom analysis and radiocarbon dating of a sediment core.

steps.description: High-resolution diatom analysis of the 1087-900 cm section of the Co1011 sediment core.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze diatom samples from sediment core

steps.description: Radiocarbon dating of the sediment core section.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon dating on sediment core

steps.description: Generate stratigraphic plots using Tilia and TGView software.
steps.searchable_summary: create stratigraphic plots with Tilia and TGView

steps.description: Perform constrained cluster analysis using CONISS.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct constrained cluster analysis with CONISS

steps.description: Ordinations of log-transformed species data using CANOCO 4.5.

methods.description: This method derives a chronology for the sediment core section using AMS radiocarbon dates and calibration curves, applying reservoir corrections and interpolating ages with a smooth spline age-depth model.

steps.description: Calibrate AMS radiocarbon dates using Calib 7.1 with SHCal13.14c and Marine13.14c calibration curves.
Apply local marine carbon reservoir effect correction of 900 years.
Apply composite reservoir effect of 665 years at lacustrine-marine transition.
Derive interpolated ages using a smooth spline age-depth model with CLAM software.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate ages with smooth spline model in CLAM
